title: The Rise of Necropolitical Oligarchy – A Global Challenge
summary: The article analyzes how raw, unashamed exercise of power is becoming normalized globally, from Putin's Russia to Trump's America, giving rise to a 'necropolitical oligarchy' of elites who govern through death, abandonment, and social exclusion. Drawing on Gramsci's concept of interregnum and Orwell's geopolitical warning in 1984, the author argues the EU may be the last hope against authoritarian drift and must pursue deeper political union to become a credible strategic power.
talking_points: Russia dropped its democratic facade as relations with the West deteriorated, openly displaying its brutal power structure
A new 'necropolitical oligarchy' of natural resources, tech, finance, security, and real estate elites is emerging in the US, governing through fear, exclusion, and environmental destruction
The Trump administration's rejection of diplomatic norms symbolically dismantles the post-WWII international order, to the satisfaction of Moscow and Beijing
Orwell's 1984 geopolitical framework of three indistinguishable superstates mirrors today's US-Russia-China triad
The EU must pursue deeper political union and common defense architecture or remain a vulnerable, reactive geopolitical actor
concepts: mass detention and deportation as governance
author: Anton Șehovțov
